basicAttack.name: Protocol α: Breach
basicAttack.description:
BASIC ATTACK: 
An attack with up to 4 sequences that deals <@ba.pulse>Electric DMG</>. As the controlled operator, <#ba.lastcombo>Final Strike</> also deals <@ba.poise>{poise:0}</> Stagger. 

DIVE ATTACK: 
Basic attack performed in mid-air becomes a dive attack that deals <@ba.pulse>Electric DMG</> to nearby enemies. 

FINISHER: 
Basic attack performed near a Staggered enemy becomes a finisher that deals massive <@ba.pulse>Electric DMG</> and recovers some SP.

battleSkill.name: Protocol ω: Strike
battleSkill.description: Unleashes a bolt of electro-magnetic energy that deals <@ba.pulse>Electric DMG</> and <#ba.pulseinflict>Electric Infliction</> to enemies in a small area.

comboSkill.name: Instant Protocol: Chain
comboSkill.description:
COMBO TRIGGER: When controlled operator performs a <#ba.lastcombo>Final Strike</> on any enemy. 
Unleashes stored electro-magnetic energy to bombard the target, dealing <@ba.pulse>Electric DMG</> and forcibly inflicting temporary <#ba.conduct>Electrification</>.

ultimate.name: Protocol ε: 70.41K
ultimate.description: Tele-protocols a massive piece of orbital debris into the battlefield with devastating impact. All enemies in the area of effect suffer massive <@ba.pulse>Electric DMG</>.

files.main:
<@profile.key>CODENAME: Perlica 
GENDER: Female 
AUTHENTICATION: Endfield Industries 
DOB: March 16 
RACE: Liberi 
[ORIPATHY INFECTION STATUS] 
Oripathy negative, based on medical examination reports. 

[INTEGRATED PHYSICAL EXAMINATION] 
PHYSIOLOGICAL STRENGTH: Standard 
COMBAT SKILL: Standard 
TACTICAL ACUMEN: Outstanding
ORIGINIUM ARTS ASSIMILATION: Excellent</>
files.humanResources:
<@profile.key>Perlica, Supervisor of Endfield Industries, is one of the official spokespersons for Endfield. She is primarily responsible for overseeing and advancing the development and application of Protocol-Originium technology protocols, while also managing the operations of the Dijiang. 

It's been a long time since I last heard anyone question whether she deserves the title of Supervisor. She has silenced all doubts with tangible results, proving M3's words true: "This position exists because of Perlica. Don't get the order wrong." I still can hardly believe someone so young is capable of supporting the management of the entire Endfield Industries. Yet under her leadership, the future that Endfield strives toward has only grown clearer. However, she isn't flawless in handling everything. For instance, as a key figure in managing the Crisis Team, she shouldn't constantly risk herself on the front lines alongside the members. Even if she continues to dismiss my requests, I will still urge her to reassess the risks. 
— Martin Marvin Malen, Assistant, HR Division, Endfield Industries</>
files.file1:
<@profile.key>Like many operators within Endfield Industries, Supervisor Perlica survived a disaster as a child and found refuge under the care of the Endministrator and Endfield Industries alongside other children who had lost their families. During her education and growth, she showed exceptional curiosity toward Techno-Protocol and gradually became involved in the foundational operations of Endfield Industries on Talos-II. In less than two years, her footsteps had reached cities both large and small across the planet, and her understanding of the threats we face far surpassed that of ordinary individuals. Her passion for Techno-Protocol led her to join the R&D Center, where she later played a key role in achieving major breakthroughs in the development and application of Techno-Protocol for Endfield Industries. Naturally, she was officially appointed as "Supervisor", taking the helm of the Dijiang—a colossal structure suspended in the artificial stationary orbit above Talos-II. 

DOCUMENT: A certain log that requires sufficient authority level 
Of course, Perlica's past was never as consistently fortunate as the public records might suggest. 
Over a decade ago, you rescued her while preparing for the expedition, offering her unwavering support at a time when she was still lost and fearful. Yet she could never have anticipated that the same crusade would soon turn her life upside down once more ... she lost you. In the years that followed, most of the children you saved alongside her moved on to new lives. This includes Lycia, whom she considered a sister, eventually left the Dijiang and chose to go her separate way. 
As friends gradually departed, the weight of responsibility on her shoulders grew heavier, and the development of Techno-Protocol repeatedly hit roadblocks... 
Yet she stubbornly remained on this most difficult path, winning us over through her actions. It's true that she received support from many along the way, but no one understands better than I do the effort she has poured in and the hardships she has endured. 
Go and have a chat with her. She's been waiting far too long.
 ——M3's Audio Memo</>
files.file2:
<@profile.key>Other operators aboard the Dijiang often say that Supervisor Perlica seems to possess a kind of magical quality. As long as she's around, any problem finds its solution. 
So when I learned that I'd have the privilege of studying alongside her, I was so excited I couldn't sleep. But reality hit me hard. Unable to keep up with her intense workload and exacting standards, I seriously considered quitting along the way … yet I couldn't bring myself to give up such a precious opportunity. 
With the expected awakening of the Endministrator just around the corner, everyone is on edge—even Supervisor Perlica is no exception. Despite her schedule being completely packed, she still manages to squeeze in meetings with senior members from every department to ensure everything goes off without a hitch. 
But after each of those impromptu private meetings, she always comes out looking uneasy. Could it be that there are things even she can't handle? I can't help but blame myself for not being able to do more to help. 
She noticed how lost I felt and took the initiative to comfort me, even sharing some strangely flavored fin meatballs I'd never seen before. The taste was so strong it brought tears to my eyes, yet Supervisor Perlica just watched me with a satisfied smile … That day's worries were washed away along with my tears. 
In that moment, I suddenly realized that Supervisor Perlica … truly does have that kind of magic. 
—From the work log of a trainee operator, who is still applying for the opportunity to continue her apprenticeship under Supervisor Perlica. 

[Record That Has Not Been Successfully Deleted] 
"She went to see you too, didn't she, Krad? Asking how to get along with the Endministrator she cares about so much, right?" 
"Wouldn't expect anything less from Intel. You're not even on the Dijiang and you're still this well-informed. When she rushed into the training room to find me, I thought something had gone wrong. But then she asked me that dead seriously. Totally took me by surprise." 
"Guess I've gotten so used to her being the Supervisor that I almost forgot she isn't actually invincible. But I do love that awkward tone she takes when she messages me. Still just as adorable as when we were kids." 
"So, what advice did you give her?" 
"Same as you, nothing at all. The other old-timers did about the same too. We're all just waiting to see how this plays out." 
"But I bet she'll hide it perfectly when she sees the Endministrator again." 
"Don't worry, M3 will definitely be with her. You really think we won't get every last detail from the scene?" 
"Right. And remember, we promised her we'd keep this completely confidential. Especially from the Endministrator." 
"Relax, Krad. You know I am the last person to talk. Professional ethics and all. But honestly, it's almost a shame we won't get to see little Perlica pouting over this, isn't it?"</>
files.file3:
<@profile.key>No matter where you run into Supervisor Perlica aboard the Dijiang, she always calmly greets you before immediately returning to her work. Some even joke that beneath her Liberi appearance, she might actually be a machine, perfectly executing the directives of some higher intelligence. While this kind of rumor spreads widely among newer operators, the Supervisor does not mind it at all. In fact, she occasionally plays along with the joke on purpose, lightening the mood and helping to ease the tension among the crew. 
According to the unofficial account by Martin Marvin Malen, after officially assuming the role of Supervisor, Perlica forced herself to adopt the most appropriate demeanor to fulfill her duties. But accumulated emotions and pressure always need an outlet. Even before the Endministrator's return, Perlica had already found a way to truly relax. "Have you heard of the 'Crankcase Creative Contest'? They say it originally started as a creative showdown between a certain Vampire genius and a mysterious mechanic. Seniors in the R&D Center brought this ancient Terra event to Endfield. Nowadays, the wild ideas that emerge from the CCC not only bring a lot of laughter to the operators, but also greatly drive our technological innovation. Yet very few know that the true force behind this event at Endfield is Supervisor Perlica. After all, she has never officially participated, and rarely voices her opinions. In truth, her reason for reviving this event was simply to create a platform where she could relieve her restless hands and test the strange ideas in her head." 

Even though I was banned from competing this year, I wouldn't call myself entirely absent. After all, I’m the one who named the award-winning "Blast-ma Roar"! When its creator came to me with the idea of "stuffing a gravity firearm into an acceleration engine", I knew it had potential. We ended up taking apart quite a few fine firearms to make it work, though… Huh? O-of course we didn't touch my dad's precious stockpile! 
—The reigning champion of multiple Crankcase Creative Contests, a prodigy in firearm engineering. 

 This vehicle just took first place in the Neo Tsukushi Underground Heat Rally! What is it doing on the Dijiang?! 
—From a racing enthusiast. 

"Blast-ma Roar"? Owned by "Ilyich, the Suspect"? Perlica, which department did this newcomer come from? 
— Chen Qianyu </>
files.file4:
<@profile.key>M3, file this document as an annex to "Zeroth Directive" under sealed status. It is not to be enforced under any circumstances. 
I hereby appoint Perlica as the "Signatory of Zeroth Directive". 
However, its activation must be entirely subject to her personal consent. 
If she ultimately decides to remain with Endfield Industries, you, as the executor, shall take her to meet the Founder. 

DOCUMENT: A certain log that requires sufficient authority level 
"Have you decided?" 
"Staying with Endfield Industries was never a question, M3." 
"Very well. Come with me. The Founder wishes to see you." 
...... 
"So we finally meet, Supervisor Perlica." 
"Su ... Supervisor? Wait, your appearance—" 
"Our time is running short."</>

potential1.name: The Guide
potential1.author: STAR影法师
potential1.description: When debris of the stars blocks the light, she will calmly illuminate your path. Look how she shines.

potential3.name: The Protocol
potential3.author: Director Kuzuri
potential3.description: Having witnessed the focus of her longing, she no longer wishes to stay put. The Protocol was never meant to be cold, unfeeling bindings.

potential6.name: The Future
potential6.author: IRple2
potential6.description: She wove an infinity of possibilities that were then etched upon the blueprints. They are the future that she always carries with her.
